data.title: Is the Gold Rally Over? Not So Fast, Says UBS
data.description:
Gold's (XAUUSD) historic rally has led to concern for some investors, who expect the precious metal's momentum to run dry. On the other side of the argument is UBS, which raised its gold price target for March, June, and September to $6,200 from $5,000.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

UBS expects the upside to be driven by central bank demand, strong exchange-traded fund (ETF) inflows, and more purchases of physical gold. The investment firm's bull case sees gold jumping to $7,200, while its bear case estimates a price of $4,600, with both targets representing a roughly one standard deviation move.

Where Will Gold Trade By the End of 2026?

At the same time, UBS expects the metal to end 2026 at $5,900, which still implies upside of about 10% from current levels. Factors to consider include whether the Fed is hawkish or dovish and the scope of geopolitical tensions.

UBS joins a growing chorus of Wall Street firms with high expectations. Earlier this week, both Deutsche Bank and Societe Generale raised their year-end price targets to $6,000.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX and xAI Are Reportedly in Talks to Merge
data.description:
Elon Musk's SpaceX and xAI are reportedly in talks to merge ahead of a major public offering planned for later this year, according to Reuters. This is noteworthy because the combined company would bring together SpaceX's rockets, Starlink satellites, the X social media platform, and xAI's Grok chatbot under one company. While the talks are still confidential and not finalized, the move could strengthen SpaceX's push into building data centers in space.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Under the proposed deal, xAI shareholders would receive SpaceX shares, and two new entities have already been created in Nevada to help facilitate the transaction. However, the exact value, timing, and structure of the merger are still unclear, and no final agreement has been signed. Some xAI executives may also have the option to receive cash instead of SpaceX stock if the deal goes through. The main reasons for the possible merger are artificial intelligence and infrastructure.

Through xAI, Musk is building a massive AI supercomputer called Colossus and has argued that putting AI data centers in space, powered by solar energy, could significantly reduce costs within a few years. In addition, bringing xAI into SpaceX could improve SpaceX's chances of winning more U.S. defense contracts, as the Pentagon increases its use of AI systems. Interestingly, SpaceX is already the world's most valuable private company, last valued at about $800 billion, and has lined up banks for an IPO that could value it at $1.5 trillion.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 11 Buys, 11 Holds, and six Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $401.27 per share implies 4.1% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Slides as Supply Chain Problems Mount
data.description:
Aerospace giant Boeing (BA) has what may be a growing problem on its hands. It is eager to build aircraft, and that is a good thing, especially given the sheer scope of its backlog. But while its spirit is willing, its flesh may not be up to the challenge. In fact, there are mounting concerns about Boeing's supply chain in a time when Boeing most needs it running at its fullest. Boeing shares slipped over 1.5% in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Boeing had a staggering earnings report, reports noted. Analysts were expecting a $0.39 per share loss, but Boeing blasted that out of the water by posting a $9.92 per share profit. Though this figure was generated mostly by a hefty one-time gain, the fact that the gain was over 25 times the figure of its projected loss had to hit home.

Boeing will need all hands on deck, though, as its supply chain starts looking stressed. Boeing recently set up a deal with USA Rare Earth that will give Boeing access to the minerals it needs to make everything from navigation systems to engines. However, if anything goes wrong with this deal, Boeing will have to turn to markets mostly run by the Chinese, which could add considerable pressure.

New Helicopter Project

And, even as Boeing works to work out its supply chain ahead of a major new production expansion, Boeing also faces a new contract from the United States Air Force for four more MH-139A helicopters. This is the second such contract it has received in the last five months, reports note, and the total value of such contracts now stands at over $262 million.

The MH-139 program's director, Azeem Khan, noted, "The quick succession of contracts demonstrates that the U.S. Air Force is all in on bringing the MH-139A capability to the warfighter as quickly as possible. Our team is dedicated to delivering and maintaining these aircraft, driven by a commitment to excellence, where safety and quality are our top priorities."

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 14 Buys, one Hold and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 34.57% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $268.69 per share implies 13.51% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: AMD vs. SMCI: Which AI Hardware Stock Do Analysts Prefer Ahead of Earnings?
data.description:

Wall Street is more bullish on AMD, which carries a Strong Buy consensus, while SMCI has a Hold rating. However, analysts see greater upside in SMCI, with price targets implying gains of about 41%, compared with roughly 14% upside for AMD.

data.title: Trump to Name Fed Chair Next Week as BlackRock's Rieder Leads the Odds
data.description:
President Trump announced on Thursday that he would name his nominee for the Fed Chair position "sometime next week."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

BlackRock's (BLK) Chief Investment Officer of Global Fixed Income, Rick Rieder, is the front-runner to be selected, according to prediction platform Polymarket. Rieder leads with 38% odds, followed by former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh at 29% and current Fed Governor Christopher Waller at 14%.

Trump Calls for Aggressive Rate Cuts

On Wednesday, the Fed voted 10-2 to maintain interest rates, much to the ire of Trump. "We should have a substantially lower rate now that even this moron admits inflation is no longer a problem or threat," Trump said in a Truth Social post earlier today, referencing Fed Chair Jerome Powell. He added that lower rates could save America billions of dollars in interest payments and that tariffs support the rationale of the U.S. having the lowest interest rate in the world.

Powell's term as Chair is set to end on May 15, although his role as a Governor extends until January 31, 2028.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Nvidia Stock Faces Pressure on Alleged Aid to China's DeepSeek AI
data.description:
American chip maker Nvidia (NVDA) is under fire from Congress for allegedly providing technical support to China's DeepSeek, which helped develop AI models potentially used by the Chinese military. In a letter, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and Rep. John Moolenaar (R-Mich.), chair of the House Select Committee on the Chinese Communist Party, accused Nvidia of offering "extensive technical support" that boosted DeepSeek's efficiency. NVDA stock fell marginally in extended trading on the news.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The committee revealed that Nvidia engineers collaborated with DeepSeek on an "optimized co-design of algorithms, frameworks, and hardware." This enabled DeepSeek-V3 to complete full training in just 2.788 million H800 GPU hours, far fewer than is typical for U.S. developers. GPU hours measure the time AI chips run to train a model; frontier-scale models are cutting-edge systems from leaders like OpenAI, Anthropic, or Alphabet's (GOOGL) Google.

Key Background of the Allegations

Last year, DeepSeek launched its low-cost, efficient R1 model, trained with fewer parameters, lower chip specifications, and cheaper resources compared to Nvidia's typical setups. The release shook global markets and sparked an AI arms race between the U.S. and China. Washington grew concerned that China was gaining an AI lead and bypassing U.S. chip export restrictions.

In 2024, DeepSeek operated as a standard commercial entity, so Nvidia treated it as a "legitimate commercial partner deserving of standard technical support," as per the letter. Nvidia had designed the H800 chip specifically for China and sold it there before U.S. export controls took effect in 2023. Reuters reported last year that U.S. officials suspect DeepSeek aids China's military while evading those controls.

Nvidia Denies Wrongdoing

Nvidia has rejected all the allegations. "China has more than enough domestic chips for all of its military applications, with millions to spare," the company told Reuters. "Just like it would be nonsensical for the American military to use Chinese technology, it makes no sense for the Chinese military to depend on American technology."

The controversy unfolds amid shifting U.S. policy. The Trump administration recently approved exports of Nvidia's H200 chips to China under strict restrictions. Yesterday, China cleared the first batch of H200 chips for import. Lawmakers criticize the decision, warning it could boost Beijing's military and erode U.S. AI dominance.

Is Nvidia Still a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, Nvidia stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $264.09 implies 37.9% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, NVDA shares have surged over 54%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: "Warning of the Burst to Come": Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Slides, Analyst Calls AI a "Planet-Sized Bubble"
data.description:
Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) is the canary in the coal mine, according to University of Michigan Ross School of Business professor Erik Gordon. Gordon believes that the recent decline in Microsoft stock is a sign of wider problems in the market, and that artificial intelligence (AI) is likely to prove a massive bubble. This idea did not help matters with investors, who sent Microsoft shares into free-fall, down nearly 12% in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Microsoft's recent plunge, Gordon noted, is a direct consequence of the "…truckloads of cash it is investing in AI." That in turn served as what he called "…a warning of the burst to come." But even with this, Gordon does not look for the bubble to burst for several months. Investors are still funneling in cash, which should keep the bubble running for some time yet, and the new technology that is emerging will prove "…exciting enough to distract…" from valuation issues.

This is not the first time Gordon has alerted on these conditions, however. He recently called AI an "…order-of-magnitude overvaluation bubble," and called upon investors to brace for suffering that would be more painful than the dot-com aftermath. Stocks, meanwhile, have refused to respond to his calls for doom.

Gaming on the Skids

Meanwhile, a key point emerged from Microsoft's recent earnings call. While the cloud business is doing quite well, and the business support operations are top-notch, the gaming division is falling apart. Gaming revenue is down 9% against this time last year, and hard questions about why are starting to emerge.

Hardware revenue is down, and first-party content is lagging a bit as well. But Microsoft looks for growth to continue through the next quarter in Game Pass, and the special events around the 25th anniversary of Xbox have yet to display their full impact. Still, with signs that Microsoft may be preparing to exit the console wars altogether in favor of a Steam Machine-style entry, it is clear that Microsoft's gaming future will not look like what it did back in 2015.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 35 Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 16.06% rally in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $604.60 per share implies 42.63% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Slips on Tech Selloff, Rising U.S. Trade Deficit
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading lower on Wednesday, although the index is holding up much better than the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) given its lower exposure to tech stocks.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, Microsoft (MSFT), which is down by over 10% after reporting its earnings, accounts for 5.34% of the Dow. Salesforce (CRM) is also bringing down the index on fears that AI will disrupt the software industry.

Meanwhile, the Commerce Department announced the U.S. trade deficit was $56.8 billion in November, well above the consensus estimate of $44 billion and nearly doubling from $29.2 billion in the prior month. Imports increased by 5% to $348.9 billion, while exports fell by 3.6% to $292.1 billion. That doesn't bode well for gross domestic product (GDP), as rising imports reduce net exports, which directly slows GDP growth.

In another negative reading, initial jobless claims for the week ended January 24 fell by 1,000 to 209,000, higher than the consensus estimate of 205,000. In addition, the Department of Labor (DOL) revised claims for the previous week to 210,000 from 200,000. Initial jobless claims serve as a gauge of layoffs and document the number of people filing for unemployment benefits for the first time.

Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by one week and track the number of people continuing to receive unemployment benefits, fell by 38,000 to 1.827 million, below the estimate of 1.850 million. The DOL revised the prior week's figure to 1.865 million from 1.849 million.

The Dow Jones is down by 0.24% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan
